value:
A Pegasus-class assault landing ship built under the Federation's Operation V mobile suit development plan.
It can enter the atmosphere, and is equipped with a Minovsky craft system that allows for planetary flight.
It is an all-purpose ship that boasts great functionallity.
The front of the ship is equipped with MS catapults, and as an MS mothership, it incorporates a number of new technologies.
During the One Year War, it served as a mothership for the Gundam, Guntank,  and Guncannon developed by Operation V.

A Principality of Zeon space cruiser that was the first warship designed with mobile suit operation in mind.
Designed to be a versatile ship from the start, it was as adept at anti-ship combat, reconnaissance, and base defense as it was at carrying MS.
In addition to a Komusai atmospheric entry pod in its bow, it could also carry landing capsules between its engines.
Several variations of this ship were produced, and it remained one of the mainline units of the Principality of Zeon's space fleet throughout the One Year War.

An Anti-Earth Union Group (AEUG) mobile cruiser inspired by the Federation's Pegasus class.
It prioritized mobile suit operations.
For that reason, it lacks the firepower of other warships, with its offense and defense handled by onboard MS.
The residential blocks on its flanks can rotate while in flight to generate artificial gravity, providing better livability compared to other ships of its time.
The addition of ballutes enables atmospheric entry and its Minovsky craft system enables atmospheric flight.
AEUG leader Blex Forer ordered this craft to be built in absolute secrecy.
It was initially called White Base II until Melanie Hue Carbine (the chairman of Anaheim Electronics and the AEUG's biggest investor) renamed it Argama.
After serving as the AEUG fleet's flagship during the Gryps Conflict, it went on to serve in the First Neo Zeon War.

A large Titans battleship built to be a symbol and a feat of strength of the organization.
It was armed with many powerful mega particle cannons and its bombardment capabilities were unrivaled at the time.
Though it was a high-performance battleship, it also had to function as a mobile suit mothership, hence the immense size.
After it was commissioned, it was temporarily entrusted to Jupitris commander Paptimus Scirocco, but it later returned to Titans command and served as their flagship.

An Anti-Earth Union Group (AEUG) mobile battleship.
The AEUG, receiving no support from the Federation Forces, constructed this new mobile suit carrier as a replacement for the mobile cruiser Argama in order to increase its fighting strength.
This vessel was designed to be a successor to the Argama-class assault cruiser and the Pegasus-class assault landing ship.
It was designed for mobile suit operation, with three catapult decks and a factory block underneath the central deck for repair and remodeling.
Another key feature is the ship's overwhelmingly powerful hyper mega particle cannon.
Its regular armament is also an improvement over the base vessel, giving it superior performance as a warship.
Thanks to its enhanced computer support capabilities, it can be operated by a very small crew.
A drum-shaped residential block and solar cells in the main wings provide further improvements.
This ship became the AEUG flagship after the Argama was damaged during the First Neo Zeon War.
Its name means "close to the Argama."

A super-heavy Neo Zeon battleship based on the design concepts of the Gwazine class used by the former Principality of Zeon.
It was built as the flagship of the Neo Zeon fleet and placed under the command of Haman Karn.
Because it was designed with Earth landings in mind, it can be equipped with a ballute for atmospheric entry, and the hull can change shape to fit within the ballute's effective radius.
Designed for all-round performance, this vessel also has excellent firepower and mobile suit support capabilities,  giving it the ability to handle nearly any kind of mission.

Designed as a mobile suit carrier based on lessons learned since the One Year War, this Federation Forces battleship was deployed in the early U.C.0090s.
The flagship of the Federation Forces's Londo Bell, it was the first vessel of the Cailum class, which was a match for the other vessels of the Universal Century.
It could carry mobile suits and perform blitzkrieg bombardment.
Combined with the adoption of heat sinks and other technological innovations, this resulted in a finished vessel that was very well balanced overall.
Because livability was not a consideration in the design, it does not possess a gravity block.
With only the bare minimum of facilities necessary for military personnel, it has almost no superfluous space.
The ship has two bridges, one for navigation and one for combat, and the central captain's seat can travel up and down between them.
The main difference between the two bridges is that there is a navigation screen in the rear of the navigation bridge, and a tactics screen on the forward bulkhead in the combat bridge, as well as a central 3D screen.
This bridge can also function as an emergency escape pod.
Many ships of this class were constructed, and they remained in active service 60 years later, at the time of the war with the Zanscare Empire in U.C.0153.

The space battleship that led the Neo Zeon fleet.
It served as the command ship of Char Aznable, supreme commander of the revived Neo Zeon forces.
This ship followed the design of the Gwazine class and Gwadan class used by the Principality of Zeon.
Its basic design was inspired by the Principality of Zeon.
Using an existing design helped to reduce construction time, the end result was better than other newly designed vessel, with reliable performance for a minimal investment.
It had two mobile suit catapults on the top and bottom of its bow, outstanding MS capacity and support features, and plenty of firepower as well.

Disguised as a civilian transport ship, this space freighter belonged to the Neo Zeon remnants known as the Sleeves, who visited the Industrial 7 colony on a special mission.
 It can carry up to four mobile suits, and also has the ability to enter the atmosphere and fly under gravity.
Originally used to make transport runs between Earth and space, it is equipped with rockets for clearing space debris, but is essentially unarmed and has virtually no combat capabilities.
This ship served as the mothership of the Sleeves' talented Garencieres Team, and fought in the conflict surrounding Laplace's Box.

A former Federation Space Force battleship that was in the hands of an anti-Federation organization.
Carris Nautilus, acting as the commander of a rebel group, entrusted it to Jamil and his crew.
In addition to a Gundam, it carries six mobile suits and a G-Falcon among its payload.
It is equipped with various weaponry, including a beam deflector in the bow, and can produce sufficient thrust to escape the atmosphere with the aid of a large booster.

A customized Archangel-class mobile assault ship used by the Earth Alliance's Atlantic Federation.
A joint development between the Alliance's Atlantic Federation and Morgenroete, Inc.
of the Orbs union, this ship is the Alliance's first-ever Mobile Suit carrier.
Developed with the aim to take the GAT-X series Mobile Suits into the fight against ZAFT's own, it comes equipped with dedicated suit facilities including a dock and two linear catapults.
Its prized weaponry includes Positron Cannon "Lohengrin" and an abundance of artillery and Missile Launchers for enhanced anti-Mobile Suit operations.
The Archangel comes equipped with a wealth of cutting-edge technology, including beam-scattering Laminated Armor and ablative gel that helps to keep it cool when entering the Earth's atmosphere.
Heavy automation also helps keep the ship operational even with a skeleton crew.
Constructed at the neutral Heliopolis colony, it was ambushed by Le Creuset's ZAFT forces immediately after its completion.
Now piloted by the few remaining original crew members and students with military aspirations, the Archangel worked to repel ZAFT's repeated attack.

A ZAFT nuclear-capable dedicated Mobile Suit space carrier.
A new kind of experimental ship designed to carry the nuclear-powered ZGMF-X series Mobile Suits.
Boasting high speeds via 10 nuclear thrusters, it leaves even the fastest of Nazca-class ships in the proverbial dust.
It addition to its Large Beam Cannons, the two METEOR cannons installed in its bow make it pack a serious punch should the need arise.
Shortly after its completion, the ship was captured by a PLANT faction under Lacus Clyne's control, becoming the core of its fleet.

A customized Archangel-class mobile assault ship used by the Earth Alliance's Atlantic Federation.
This ship is the second version of its high performance predecessor, the Archangel.
Not much has changed compared to the Archangel aside from the appearance of its bridge sensors and the layout of its Mobile Suit maintenance deck.
Shortly after its deployment to the Alliance Lunar Base, it was assigned three second-generation GAT-X Mobile Suits and ordered to pursue the Archangel which had defected from the Alliance.

A ZAFT planetary assault landing vessel.
This speedy battleship was developed to carry the new Second Stage Series Mobile Suits. 
Equipment including dedicated catapults as well as a Deuterion Beam Energy Transfer System used to recharge Second Series suits makes it a highly effective support ship. 
It also packs an impressively high amount of firepower, with 2-Barrel Beam Cannons ""Tristan,"" 42cm Cannons ""Isolde,"" as well as a high-output Positron Cannon ""Tannhauser.""
Though anchored at ZAFT's Armory One in anticipation of its launch, it was immediately put into service inhopes of rescuing a set of stolen Gundams.

A warship commissioned by the Federation in A.G.115.
With two large decks and each one equipped with a catapult, its structure was designed with mobile suit deployment in mind.
Shortly after its commission, it was enhanced by the Madorna Workshop using data provided by the AGE System, and equipped with a photon blaster cannon and the means to transform into "assault landing mode."

A Tekkadan armored assault ship.
The ship is powered by the same Ahab Reactor used in the Calamity War.
Formerly known as the Will-O'-the-Wisp, it was owned by CGS President Maruba Arkay.
Once seized by Tekkadan, it was rechristened the Isaribi and became the group's flagship.
Though equipped with multiple weapons including 2-Barrel Cannons and Anti-Air Cannons, its Nanolaminate Armor's damage resistance makes it especially suited to be a well-armored spear point in a charge.
It's equipped with Mobile Suit launchers in its belly, allowing it to serve as a Mobile Suit carrier as well as use the Alaya-Vijnana System for enhanced control.

An armed transport vessel owned by Tekkadan.
Given a new task in keeping with Tekkadan's expansion, this ship has the ability to make the trip from Mars to Earth in around three weeks.
It is composed of a main armored assault core with container blocks on each side. These containers are equipped with hangars with a capacity for numerous Mobile Suits. 
While the core of the ship is loaded with Anti-Air Cannons and Missiles, the addition of the containers meant sacrificing the main guns on its flanks.

An all-purpose warship with a wide range of uses, from a training ship for teams with little live combat experience to a carrier for mercenary teams to get from one battlefield to another.
Equipped with Anti-Air Autocannons, missiles, and linked Beam Cannons all over, it has plenty of firepower in addition to mobility and Mobile Suit storage capacity, making it a well-balanced all-rounded ship for many situations.
However, it has an undeniably low performance relative to standard warships, and due to this, it is often treated as a stopgap ship to be used only until a better option is available.
It uses a wide variety of technology in all aspects, but its relationship with the military and the story behind its development are unclear.
